verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a purely secreted glycoprotein with a signal peptide and no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or intrinsic membrane-anchoring motif. While it associates with sperm and egg plasma membranes during fertilization, these are reversible non-covalent interactions that remain in equilibrium with the secreted pool. The protein body lacks a stable physical link to the cell surface membrane—it is recruited only by complementary binding to surface receptors on gametes, failing the recruitment test. Classification: secreted protein, not surface-accessible.
